description_long: This clock tower is a tall, rectangular structure with a beige facade and distinct horizontal banding, reminiscent of the iconic Big Ben in London. The tower features a prominent, circular clock face with black hour and minute hands, but no visible numerals or markings, set into the upper section of the building. Above the clock face, the structure narrows and transitions into a steep, gray pyramidal spire with a darker base and small vertical slits suggesting ventilation or bell openings. The lower portion of the tower is segmented by horizontal ledges, giving it a tiered appearance. The overall design is simple yet stately, with minimal ornamentation and a focus on geometric forms. The materials and color palette evoke a sense of stone or concrete construction, suitable for a public landmark in an urban environment.
description: A tall, beige clock tower with a circular clock face and a steep gray pyramidal spire, featuring horizontal banding and minimal ornamentation.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full clock face, central vertical alignment, and the prominent spire above.
description_view_1: Slightly rotated view with the clock face still visible, revealing more of the left side's banding and spire details.
description_view_2: Further rotated view, clock face remains visible, with the right side's banding and spire edges more pronounced.
description_view_3: Nearly rear view, but the clock face is still visible; the spire and banding are consistent with other views.
attribute_clock_face_design: The clock face is circular, white, and lacks numerals or minute markings. It features two black hands (hour and minute) with a simple, minimalist design and no decorative elements.
